rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Setting Expert Disclosure Date Ccp Section 2034.250(A) And (B), Or In The Alternative Relief For Late Disclosure (Ccp Section 2034.720) And For An Order That Claimant Submit To A Medical Examination (Ccp Section 2024050)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 7, PETITIONER CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion For An Order Setting Expert Disclosure Date CCP Section 2034.250(A) And (B), Or In The Alternative Relief For Late Disclosure (CCP Section 2034.720) And For An Order That Claimant Submit To A Medical Examination (CCP Section 2024050). Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This motion was specially set for hearing on shortened time on September 6, 2019. Opposition to the motion was required to be served on or before September 13, 2019. No opposition has been received by the court or posted in the Register of Actions. The motion is decided as unopposed. Petitioner's motion to serve its Expert Witness List is granted with Petitioner's Disclosure of Expert Witnesses dated August 28, 2019 deemed timely served. Respondent may serve a Supplemental Expert Witness List within ten days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. Also granted is Petitioner's demand for a neurologic medical examination by Dr. Strassberg on October 7, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FORCE OF NATURE, INC. ET AL VS. SHEA LABAGH DOBBERSTEIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS JAMES DOBBERSTEIN, EDWARD HANLEY, SHEA LABAGH DOBBERSTEIN CERTIFIED PUBLIC ACCOUNTANTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for discretionary appointment of a Discovery Referee pursuant to C.C.P. 639(a)(5) is denied. The Court's discretion requires application of Rule of Court 3.920(c) which provides no appointment shall be made absent a finding of exceptional circumstances. The underlying factual and legal issues in this accountant malpractice action are not especially complex and substantial discovery has been completed. Previous discovery motions filed by plaintiffs reflect the parties' robust prefiling meet and confer process which successfully narrowed discovery issues. A deposition notice directed to Peter Klapper and Force of Nature's person most qualified was recently served and the parties are now actively conferring regarding the scope of testimony and documents to be produced. There is no reason to assume they will not be successful. The Court does find sufficient complexity or exceptional circumstance to warrant Discovery Referee participation in deposition scheduling, scope of inquiry, document production or active questioning. Noted is the Discovery Order dated July 1, 2019 and its detailed direction for discovery in connection with plaintiffs' allegation of defendant's willful spoliation of documentary evidence. There is insufficient complexity of the facts and law surrounding the purportedly missing documents to support the exceptional circumstance requirement of Rule of Court 3.920(c). Multiple motions to compel filed in the past by plaintiffs as a basis for a finding of necessity for appointment of a Discovery Referee going forward is not helpful to defendants because the motions have all been granted except the one denied without prejudice to refiling on completion of the court ordered focused discovery referenced above. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER YEH VS. CARUMA TECHNOLOGIES INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Hayden Ai Technologies' [Ex Parte] Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against It [Per 9/19 Ost]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 12, Defendant Hayden Ai Technologies' [Ex Parte] Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against It. Defendant Hayden AI Technologies, Inc.'s motion to vacate the default entered against it on September 13, 2019 is granted, and the September 30, 2019 trial date is vacated. This action was filed on February 28, 2018. Hayden was first named in the second amended complaint filed on July 11, 2019, and was served on August 7, 2019. On September 6, 2019, Hayden retained counsel to represent it, who immediately contacted plaintiff's counsel and requested a 30-day extension of time to respond to the complaint on its behalf, and also suggested that counsel discuss continuing the September 30, 2019 trial date. Plaintiff's counsel would agree only to a one-day continuance for Hayden to respond to the complaint, and refused to discuss continuing the trial date. Because Hayden was not served until shortly before the trial date, plaintiff's counsel's refusal to grant it a reasonable extension of time to respond to the complaint effectively precluded the parties from meeting and conferring regarding the grounds for a possible demurrer to the complaint. Further, Hayden acted diligently to seek a continuance of the trial date and an extension of time to respond to the second amended complaint. Under the circumstances, the default must be set aside. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 430.41(a)(2), 473(b).) The Court expressly disapproves plaintiff's counsel's unprofessional and uncivil conduct in, among other things, refusing to grant opposing counsel a reasonable extension of time to respond to the second amended complaint, refusing to meet and confer regarding the grounds for a possible demurrer, and refusing to agree to a continuance of the trial date, despite having first named and served Hayden just weeks before that date. (See Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.4th 127.) The Court expects better of counsel. Hayden shall have 30 days following entry of this order to respond to the second amended complaint. If it elects to file a demurrer, the parties are ordered to meet and confer in person or by telephone for the purpose of determining whether an agreement can be reached that would resolve the questions to be raised in the demurrer. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 430.41(a).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SESL RECOVERY, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. DEUTSCHE BANK SECURITIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Pending Appeal Of Remand Ruling
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK SECURITIES, INC. Motion To Stay Pending Appeal Of Remand Ruling. Defendant Deutsche Bank Securities Inc.'s motion to stay pending its appeal of an order remanding this action from the United States Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York to this Court is granted. It would promote judicial economy to stay the action until it can be determined whether the action will be heard in the Southern District of New York, pursuant to the order of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation that it be coordinated or consolidated with the ongoing multidistrict litigation relating to the bankruptcy of SunEdison, Inc., or whether it will be heard in this Court. The Court understands that Deutsche Bank is already a defendant in the SunEdison MDL proceedings. The Court further understands that the parties have reached an agreement pursuant to which discovery in this action would be stayed until the earlier of December 31, 2019 or a decision on Deutsche Bank's appeal. (Opp. at 1.) A limited stay pending the disposition of Deutsche Bank's appeal would prevent potentially inconsistent pretrial rulings, and would conserve the Court's and the parties' resources. Plaintiff does not identify any concrete prejudice it would suffer as a result of the requested stay. (See Freiberg v. City of Mission Viejo (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1484, 1489 ["Trial courts generally have the inherent power to stay proceedings in the interests of justice and to promote judicial efficiency."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: GRAHAM NELSON VS. FAIR SERVICING, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF GRAHAM NELSON MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Plaintiff Graham Nelson's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in the amount of $11,575.49 in attorneys' fees and $750.45 in costs. The Court finds that the amount of the award plaintiff is excessive for the quality and nature of the work performed, and that not all of the time spent by plaintiff's counsel was "necessarily expended" to enforce defendant's liability under the Rosenthal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. (Civ. Code § 1788.30(c).) This finding is based on the Court's review of the record and of counsel's time records, taking into account among other things counsel's expertise in the subject matter, defendants' early settlement offers, and the fact that one attorney billed (albeit at a lower hourly rate) for clerical tasks such as filing and service. The Court will not award any fees for travel to or attendance at the hearing on the fees motion. The Court finds that calculation of a reasonable lodestar requires a 25% reduction of the remaining hours for which compensation is sought (46 hours), to 34.5 hours. The Court further finds that plaintiff has made a sufficient showing as to the reasonable hourly rates for counsel's time, and that defendants have not rebutted that showing. The amount resulting from these adjustments, which the Court finds to be the reasonable lodestar, is $11,575.49. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. EDUARDO O MONTEAGUDO JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order That Matters In Req. For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'s Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion for an Order that Matters in Request for Admission of Truth of Fact be Admitted is unopposed and granted. A copy of this Order with Notice of Entry shall be served on defendant within twenty days. A copy of the Requests for Admissions referenced in the motion shall be attached to the Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VANESSA BUSTOS VS. COFFEE MEETS BAGEL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Send A Bellaire Notice To The Putative Class Members
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFF VANESSA BUSTOS Motion To Send A Bellaire Notice To The Putative Class Members. Plaintiff's motion for an order requiring Defendants to send out the attached opt-out notice to all individuals whom Coffee Meets Bagel designated as independent contractors or employee consultants since May 6, 2016, is granted. A representative plaintiff's access to the contact information of other potential class members is "is within the proper scope of discovery, an essential first step to prosecution of any representative action." (Williams v. Superior Court (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 544). Although there are privacy considerations with distributing the contact information of current and former employees, those concerns are properly addressed by way of an opt-out notice. (Belaire-West Landscape, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 554, 562 ("Requiring current and former employees to object to disclosure of their identities and contact information presents no serious invasion of their privacy interests").) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: REDWOOD ELECTRIC GROUP, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DPR CONSTRUCTION, A GENERAL PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT DPR CONSTRUCTION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant DPR Construction's demurrer to Plaintiff Redwood Electric Group, Inc.'s complaint is overruled. A demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack; or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) The Court declines to judicially notice the findings, conclusions, and decision of the hearing officer at the prior substitution hearing or Redwood's hearing brief in connection with that hearing. DPR has not shown that the factual findings in the prior Public Contract Code section 4107 hearing are properly subject to judicial notice. (See JMS Air Conditioning & Appliance Service, Inc. v. Santa Monica Community College Dist. (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 945, 959 ["the findings in the Substitution Decision do not 'possess a judicial character' and thus have no binding effect in non-section 4107 civil or administrative actions."]; id. at fn. 5 ["because we affirm the Substitution Decision, [the subcontractor] is precluded from suing for violation of section 4107. But this preclusion does not apply to any other actions or defenses."].) Further, "a section 4107 substitution hearing does not adjudicate anything beyond those limited rights section 4107 created. [] Thus, a subcontractor . . . remains free to pursue claims against the prime contractor that are not based on section 4107 statutory rights or duties. For example, if [the subcontractor] believes it was wrongfully terminated under the terms of the subcontract with [the general contractor], it may seek redress under the terms of that contract." (Id. at 958; see also Knickerbocker v. City of Stockton (1988) 199 Cal.App.3d 235, 245-246 [trial court erred in sustaining demurrer without leave to amend where adverse determination in civil service hearing was not fatal to all of plaintiff's causes of action].) As to the second cause of action, which seeks to state a claim for violation of section 4107, the Court takes judicial notice that Plaintiff has filed a petition for administrative mandate of mandate challenging the hearing officer's decision. "A hearing officer's decision is not final and binding where it is reviewable by a trial court under Code of Civil Procedure section 1094.5." (American Federation of State, County & Municipal Employees v. Metropolitan Water Dist. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 247, 259; see generally Lucido v. Superior Court (1990) 51 Cal.3d 335, 340 [essential element of collateral estoppel is that "the decision in the former proceeding must be final and on the merits"].) Plaintiff's complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support the first, second, third, and fifth causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
